notes: Starting without problems. I had crackling sound after a minute in the main menu. Editing the /etc/pulse/daemon.pa fixed it for me. I uncommented (remove semicolon) 'default-fragments = 4' and 'default-fragment-size-msec = 25' in that file. Then restart pulseaudio with 'pulsaudio -k' and 'pulseaudio --start'. Then I had no crackling sounds any more.

notes:
I was considering rating this games functionality at Gold-to-Silver-rate...but it happened not to be in protons' realm, per se, the problem(s) which caused the character models to be invisible. 
Actually tried a fair number of tweaks, options, and multiple proton versions.
-And almost posted here the dissapointing results, until I was filling out the form with "System info" when the parsing came back with my driver version in the red. [Stock -Nvidia-proprietary-drivers-390.* for Linux Mint 19.1 "Tessa"] 
   So, not wanting my results to be lacking; I sudo'ed: "add-apt-repository ppa:graphics-drivers/ppa" updated to 418-nvidia-drivers, emptied all the 'Launch options' for testing, and ended up playing for multiple runs; 
--Thus, here we are: going for the platinum.

notes:
Game runs perfectly out of the box, just make sure you have the latest 418 Nvidia proprietary drivers installed. The game will not display models if you are using the current Debian Stretch/Buster/Sid version 410.104 or older. 
Debian users can easily upgrade to the latest Nvidia drivers by installing the experimental nvidia-driver package, from the official experimental Debian repository using apt.

title: Risk of Rain 2

notes:
Without any options changed, the Steam will start installing VC redist or whatever, and then a window will pop up titled "Risk of Rain 2 - 2018.3.0f2..." with a progress bar and a logo.  This window will then disappear after the progress bar reaches the end, and then nothing happens other than Steam saying the game is running.  Nothing in the task manager says the actual Risk of Rain 2 executable is running.  The first several times I tried this, the only way to get Steam to stop saying the game is running was to manually end explorer.exe, which did not end any of the other wine processes.  These other wine processes for the most part didn't end even after closing out of Steam.  

I then tried other startup options (disable Esync, disable D3d11, and use Wine D3d11).  Disabling Esync didn't change anything.  Disabling D3d11 only made it such that Steam would immediately stop saying the game was running after the progress bar window closed.  Using Wine D3d11 did allow the game to start, albeit in an unplayable state.  At the title screen and character select, the buttons and menus would show, but absolutely nothing in the background (no 3D models, no backgrounds) would render.   The background was absolutely black.
title: Risk of Rain 2

notes:
Looks near-perfect. A weird-looking pattern is rendered when hovering over items you can interact with. Other than that, nothing strange going on. Cannot set refresh rate above 60Hz, however.

Unsure about performance; no support for screens above 60Hz, so it's hard to judge if performance is affected or I'm too used to high refresh rates.

For sound, I had to set the backend to pulseaudio with winetricks.

WINESERVER="$HOME/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/Proton\ 4.2/dist/bin/wineserver" WINEPREFIX="$HOME/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/632360/pfx" winetricks

notes: As far as I could test, everything works; I get worse performance on Linux than I get on the same machine on Windows (unstable 30fps 720p vs smoother 30fps 1080p, higher settings on Windows than on Linux too). My laptop isn't a powerhouse though.
title: Risk of Rain 2

notes:
Crashes on exit since Scorched Acres update. Didn't notice anything else being broken. 
Played about an hour of 3 player multiplayer sessions with mixed characters with windows users.
title: Risk of Rain 2

notes: Runs perfectly out of the box, about as smooth as in Windows
title: Risk of Rain 2

notes: crashes often on exit, played on low gfx settings too get good perf in loaded scenes...
title: Risk of Rain 2

notes: Music is perfect, sound is perfect, UI/Menu is perfect, but everything else is broken. Its just an all black screen save the few menu icons. Starting a new run works as expected, and due to sound queues I can only assume that i am "playing" the game, except I don't see any of the environment or terrain. It used to not even run, but something changed in the scorched acres update and now its starting up. Its still unplayable
title: Risk of Rain 2

notes: Game was working perfectly until today when either a game update or Proton update borked multiplayer. One Proton update later and not only does multiplayer work again, but the game also no longer crashes on exiting. Other than that, there have been no issues spending most of my playtime (90 hours) in co-op with windows users.
title: Risk of Rain 2

notes: Character models won't render. Runs fine otherwise, but since both the player character and enemies are invisible it's not really playable.
title: Risk of Rain 2

notes: It runs more or less perfectly but upon exiting the game it usually stops responding.
title: Risk of Rain 2

notes:
Risk of Rain 2 is "running" according to the Steam library, but no window pops up and nothing happens after.  Other games in my library seem to working using Proton.

title: Risk of Rain 2

notes: Just gonna add to my previous report that this game doesn't work with proton 4.2-9 and it's corresponding DXVK version which I believe to be 1.2.1, you need a newer DXVK version like 1.3 for the game to work. Here's my solution: https://www.reddit.com/r/linux_gaming/comments/ccsra8/dxvk_13_released/etpbn4a?utm_source=share&utm_medium=web2x

notes: I was expecting game not to start at all considering previous reports for that version of Proton, but the game just runs flawlessly. No bug whatsoever, and really smooth rendering/experience.

title: Risk of Rain 2

notes: The game has issues loading player and enemy models as well as terrain elements, all other aspects of the game are present. The game still shows all UI elements; damage numbers, health bars, equipment and prices. I had tried using different solutions through Wine, but i had no success. This makes the game wholly unplayable as you can only locate yourself through your weapons, enemy projectiles and health bars. Though this sounds like an interesting premise as a game, it isn't what was Intended.
title: Risk of Rain 2

notes: Works flawlessly. Framerate drops heavy in some levels compared to others (from 100 down to around 25FPS during boss fights in the magma level) but I believe this is because the game isn't fully optimized yet. I play multiplayer with my Windows friends and they also have the same frame drops, we've all seen as low as the single digits FPS.
title: Risk of Rain 2

notes: Runs flawlessly, although there seems to be a slight input delay, which isn't significant enough to hurt the experience.
title: Risk of Rain 2

notes: High volumes of enemies can reduce FPS considerably when compared to windows, but it's no slouch, almost perfect.

notes: It's just the perfect plug and play experience. works like a charm without any performance problems. 10/10 would linux again

notes: Mine works fine but, iv seen others having problems, look for proton settings to get more frames and update graphics drivers if characters are invisible. Otherwise the game runs just as well and sometimes better than on windows.

notes: I've tried the tweaks posted throughout ratings that vary from borked to platinum, I've tried running older versions of proton, nothing worked. This game just won't go past clicking the play button and claiming it's running for a split second.
title: Risk of Rain 2

notes: I didn't need to run any tweaks, I previously wrote a review of this saying it wasn't working, but I made a mistake. It was completely my fault. Make if you're using a secondary hard drive, that it is formatted to ext4!
